location.office_time_required: 3 days in the office per week

level_signals: 5+ years of experience in program management for major projects
Sole ownership of end-to-end delivery of large-scale GPU clusters
Provides executive visibility on portfolio of concurrent programs
Expected to drive durable fixes across tooling, process, and partner interfaces

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 5+ years of experience in program management for major projects including capital projects or hyperscaler infrastructure deployment.
must_haves.qualifier: including capital projects or hyperscaler infrastructure deployment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Lead end-to-end delivery of both New Compute SKUs and large-scale GPU clusters across an external partner ecosystem while supporting capacity planning for training and inference.
Ability to contextually drive multi-threaded bring-up programs spanning hardware, networking, power, and cooling—owning plans, dependencies, and critical paths.
Build and operationalize program mechanisms (roadmaps, milestones, risk registers, runbooks) that make delivery predictable at massive scale.
Are comfortable managing cross-functional and cross-company teams; experience driving information and decision hygiene.
Have experience interfacing with and leading external vendors including engineering firms, equipment suppliers, and/or construction firms.
Have an extensive track record of successfully delivering high-profile, technical projects against tight deadlines.
Are technically adept and have effectively partnered with engineering or fundamental research teams of the highest caliber.
Have experience managing complicated dependencies such as logistics and/or supply chains.
Have expertise in designing and implementing simple, scalable processes that solve complex problems.
Are relentlessly resourceful and thrive in ambiguous, fast-paced environments.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Possess a degree in a hard science, or have a demonstrated track record of engineering expertise.
Interface with chip providers to derisk long-term onboarding to new hardware platforms by working across kernels, comms, hardware, and scheduling engineering teams.

Support network operations and end-to-end physical and logical bring-up of OpenAI network Points-of-Presence (PoPs), including on-site deployment, rack cabling, and close collaboration with engineering teams.
Are interested in and thoughtful about the impacts of AGI.

scope.surface: GPU fleet and large-scale compute clusters (training and inference) for ChatGPT and the API
scope.business_model: AI research and deployment company; internal infrastructure enabling production Applied AI and Research training workloads
primary_metrics_inferred: On-time delivery of GPU clusters and compute SKUs
Executive-level portfolio visibility and risk mitigation
cross_functional_partners: External vendors (engineering firms, equipment suppliers, construction firms)
core_responsibilities: Lead end-to-end delivery of New Compute SKUs and large-scale GPU clusters across external partner ecosystem
Drive multi-threaded bring-up programs spanning hardware, networking, power, and cooling
Interface with chip providers to derisk onboarding to new hardware platforms
Build and operationalize program mechanisms (roadmaps, milestones, risk registers, runbooks)
Partner with engineering to improve cluster turn-up reliability and automation
Support network operations and physical/logical bring-up of OpenAI network PoPs
Coordinate cross-functional readiness to ship production-ready compute
Manage integration and handoffs across teams and partners
Identify bottlenecks and systemic gaps and drive durable fixes
Provide executive visibility on progress, tradeoffs, and risks

green_flags: Clear and specific role scope — owns end-to-end GPU cluster delivery at OpenAI scale
Engineer-first TPM team culture described explicitly
Relocation assistance offered to new employees
Hybrid model with defined cadence (3 days/week) rather than vague policy
Role touches cutting-edge AI infrastructure (ChatGPT and API serving + next-gen model training)
red_flags: Visa sponsorship status not mentioned anywhere in the JD
On-site presence in San Francisco required (3 days/week) — eliminates remote candidates
Breadth of scope (hardware, networking, power, cooling, logistics, vendor management, chip providers, PoPs) is extremely wide and may reflect understaffing or high execution pressure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ years requirement, ownership language, and executive visibility expectations; not stated as a level title)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no direct reports mentioned)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description and team context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned (no mention of equity in JD)
ambiguities: The 'You might thrive in this role if you:' section header introduces all requirements, making hard/soft classification ambiguous. Items phrased as direct experience claims ('Have 5+ years...', 'Have experience interfacing with...') were treated as hard must-haves. Items more discretionary in nature ('Possess a degree in a hard science, or have a demonstrated track record...' and 'Are interested in and thoughtful about the impacts of AGI') were treated as nice-to-haves.
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; marked as 'unclear'.

The chip provider interfacing and PoP deployment bullets appear in the 'In this role, you will:' responsibilities section rather than the requirements section — they are role activities, not stated requirements. Captured as nice-to-haves reflecting desirable domain experience.
